zotero.abstractNote: Learning complex motor skills with robots is a challenging task, especially when the robot is actuated by non-linear, compliant actuators such as pneumatic artificial muscles. These actuators are difficult to control due to their non-linear dynamics, hysteresis, and time-varying properties. In this paper, we present a framework for learning to play table tennis from scratch with a robot actuated by pneumatic artificial muscles. We combine a model-based approach for low-level control with a model-free reinforcement learning approach for high-level task learning. We demonstrate that our approach allows the robot to learn to play table tennis in a real-world setting, starting from scratch, without any prior knowledge of the robot's dynamics or the task. We evaluate our approach in a series of experiments, showing that the robot can learn to hit the ball consistently and accurately, and that it can adapt to changes in the environment.
